articles.title: 'I feel so grateful to be alive': Meningitis survivor speaks to the BBC
articles.description: Annabelle Mackay, 21, has spoken to the BBC about her experience surviving meningitis.

articles.title: Record-breaking heat wave grips western US
articles.description: A record early heat wave striking the west of the United States on Friday is a one-in-500-years type event and all but certainly the result of human-caused climate change, experts say.

articles.title: 'Trump could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment,' ex-adviser John Bolton says

articles.title: Border security chief to step down after 18 months in the job
articles.description: Martin Hewitt had been tasked with cutting the numbers trying to get to the UK in small boats.

articles.title: Wheat's immune system uses novel protein complex, study finds

articles.title: Two new fungi species uncovered in Australian herbarium using DNA sequencing

articles.title: Palestinians celebrate Eid in Gaza, making the most of a fragile ceasefire
articles.description: The ceasefire, in effect for the past six months, has brought some reprieve to Palestinians in Gaza despite continued hardship, displacement and Israeli restrictions on aid.

articles.title: Nottingham triple killer should have been arrested before attacks, police chief admits
articles.description: The former Nottinghamshire Police Chief Constable Kate Meynell has given evidence to the Nottingham Inquiry.

articles.title: Luke Littler applies to trademark his face to combat AI fakes
articles.description: The darts star's image has been used legally on darts merchandise, snacks and fashion brands.

articles.title: Iran war: Assassinations leave little room for negotiation
articles.description: With most of its leaders killed, those stepping up to take their place in the Iranian regime appear to be far less willing to compromise. Is Washington's strategy of decapitation strikes backfiring?

articles.title: Kinsky set to return as Vicario awaits hernia surgery
articles.description: Tottenham goalkeeper Guglielmo Vicario will have surgery on a hernia problem, paving the way for Antonin Kinsky to return to the side.

articles.title: Vertical gardens prove effective in improving indoor air quality
articles.description: Researchers at the University of Seville have demonstrated the effectiveness of active vertical garden systems in improving indoor air quality in buildings. To do so, they worked inside a closed glass chamber installed at the Higher Technical School

articles.title: Stocks sink on fears the war with Iran will keep interest rates high
articles.description: Stocks sink on fears the war with Iran will keep interest rates high  AP News

articles.title: Civilians that were under siege by a Mexican cartel fight back with AK-47s and grenades
articles.description: Experts say Guerrero now holds many competing armed groups and that vigilantes are often absorbed, and armed, by the cartels themselves.

articles.title: Iranian man arrested trying to enter Faslane nuclear base
articles.description: A 34-year-old man and a 31-year-old woman were arrested at HM Naval Base Clyde, which houses UK's nuclear-armed submarines, on Thursday.

articles.title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
articles.description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

articles.title: Reform suspends Scotland candidate less than a day after he was announced

articles.title: New BTS album drops as Seoul gets ready for comeback mega-gig

articles.title: Low-cost test dissolves threat of fake drugs
articles.description: Fake news can be tricky to spot, but spotting fake drugs just got a little easier. Researchers have devised a low-cost way to help distinguish legitimate medications from counterfeit ones.

articles.title: The Largest Coffee Cup in Colombia in Chinchiná, Colombia

articles.title: Reeves to Lose £3 Billion of Fiscal Buffer Amid Gilts Selloff
articles.description: The surge in UK gilt yields on the back of the Iran war could cost Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves more than a 10th of her fiscal buffer by increasing the cost of servicing government debt, according to Bloomberg economists.

articles.title: Mola Museum (MuMo) in Panamá City, Panama

articles.title: MI5 contractor who gave intelligence to foreign power 'was insane'
articles.description: Juan Joseph, a former MI5 IT contractor, shared information with a foreign state but was found "not guilty by reason of insanity" by a jury.

articles.title: 'Harm at a population level': World Happiness Report flags social media's negative impact

articles.title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
articles.description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the c

articles.title: Bailey faces uphill battle in Illinois governor’s race against Pritzker

articles.title: Bond Market’s Big 2026 Fed Bet Flipped on Its Head by Oil Surge
articles.description: Bond traders are scrambling for a new strategy after the oil-driven inflation shock triggered by the war in Iran scuppered the popular bet on further interest-rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.
